CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, value INT);
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE value < (SELECT MAX(value) FROM test_table);
SELECT * FROM test_table t1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM test_table t2 WHERE t1.value < t2.value);
SELECT t1.id, t1.value FROM test_table t1 JOIN test_table t2 ON t1.value < t2.value;
SELECT AVG(value) FROM test_table WHERE value < 3;
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE value < 3);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE value < 4;
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT, ref_id INT);
SELECT a.id FROM another_table a JOIN test_table t ON a.ref_id < t.id WHERE t.value < 2;
SELECT t1.id FROM test_table t1, test_table t2 WHERE t1.value < t2.value AND t2.id = 1;
CREATE VIEW less_than_two AS SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE value < 2;
